what_it_does: Exports a React component that renders an addressed history/audit view for the currently indicated ui:// element. It reads indicated/history/historyBusy from AppContext, groups the trajectory events by kind into collapsible sections, and displays each event using shared event-row styling.
observations: Uses SectionHead and Badge from ../components/kit for the region head.
Consumes useApp context values: indicated, history, historyBusy.
Maintains local collapse state keyed by event kind so it survives re-fetches.
Returns null unless indicated exists and starts with ui://.
Groups events by e.kind, preserving backend chronological order within each group.
Sorts kind groups by descending event count.
Reuses existing .ev / .ev-k / .ev-s / .ev-t CSS classes for event rows.
data-ui-ref is set on the outer container as ui://inspector/history.
Shows an empty-state message when there are no events and loading is not busy.
The head conversion is described as a light touch: only SectionHead/Badge changed, body markup preserved.
standouts: History is grouped by kind into collapsible sections rather than rendered as a flat log.
Group sort is by most-active kind first, with newest timestamp shown per group.
Default collapse state leaves all groups open so the operator sees the whole shape at a glance.
The head was converted to a commander's-bridge kit SectionHead while the existing event-row body vocabulary was intentionally left untouched.
conventions: React functional component with local useState.
Conditional null return for unsupported/non-ui:// loci.
Design-token / shared-class styling rather than hardcoded colors.
Uses relTime utility for relative timestamps.
Imports kit components for the region chrome.
concerns: groups is typed as Record<string, any[]> with no event shape typing visible in this file.
Keys use e.seq ?? i, so fallback index keys can appear if seq is missing.
Sorting groups by count reorders the discovery order of kinds, which may obscure chronological narrative across kinds.
Only historyBusy and traj.length drive UI feedback; no explicit error or retry state is shown.
notes: A read-only, addressed history region that surfaces an element's trajectory through kind-grouped, collapsible event rows with a kit-styled head.
questions: What is the exact event schema beyond kind, summary, ts, and seq?
How does AppContext fetch and refresh history for the indicated locus?
What is the complete set of event kinds the backend can return?
Are there accessibility requirements for the hist-group-head button/caret behavior?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the implementation: it shows an addressed history view, grouped by kind, only for ui:// indicated elements, reading from the runtime via AppContext.
intention_signals: Make the runtime's addressed history inspectable at a specific UI locus without presenting a flat log, while keeping the region visually coherent with Activity/Versions/SelfChanges by reusing the shared event-row vocabulary.
novelty: The kind-grouped, collapsible, most-active-first history layout, combined with a head-only conversion to the commander's-bridge kit language while preserving the legacy event-row body markup.
contribution: A reusable addressed-history region pattern that combines a kit-styled head with a preserved, shared event-row body, contributing grouped, navigable audit presentation to the surface.
summary_for_embedding: History.tsx exports a React component that renders a collapsible, kind-grouped addressed history/audit view for an indicated ui:// element using shared event-row styling.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a navigable, design-coherent history region that shows everything that happened at a specific UI address without owning the underlying runtime data.

symbols.does: Renders an addressed history/audit region for the currently indicated ui:// element.
symbols.description: The History component consumes indicated, history, and historyBusy from AppContext, returns null unless the indicated locus starts with ui://, groups trajectory events by kind, sorts groups by descending size, and renders collapsible sections of shared .ev event rows behind a kit SectionHead and Badge aside.
